summary: I listed /data and found gps.json, locations.json and time_logs.csv, attempted to read those files and inspect their contents (time_logs.csv partially visible showing entries mentioning Rafał), and tried to run Python to parse files but the interpreter was not available. Several file reads were truncated due to large output limits and some attempts returned FileNotFoundError.
error: Output truncated due to size limits; FileNotFoundError when attempting to open some files (/data/time_logs.csv and /data/gps.json reported missing in later attempts); 'python: not found' when trying to run a parser.
lesson: When working on the server, use streaming/limited-output commands (head, tail, grep, jq) to avoid huge outputs and to locate relevant lines before full reads. Verify file paths from a fresh ls and use available tools (e.g., sh utilities, awk, jq) if python is not installed. If a file appears in an initial listing but later access fails, re-check current working directory, permissions, and exact filenames/casing before reattempting.

summary: Listed the /data directory, searched time_logs.csv for entries mentioning "Rafał", then mapped the found entry's location_id through locations.json and gps.json to obtain the city and coordinates.
lesson: Be more targeted to avoid oversized outputs: use grep -n or awk to locate matching CSV rows, pipe to head/tail to preview, and use jq with a filter (e.g. jq '.[] | select(.location_id==NNN)') to directly extract coordinates. When joining CSV and JSON data, write a small script or use join/python to reliably map entry -> location -> gps so outputs are complete and reproducible.
